title: Union Table
premise: A newly formed union at a manufacturing plant is negotiating its first contract. Both the union and management are under pressure from their respective hardliners, and a strike deadline looms despite neither side wanting a work stoppage.
setting: A windowless conference room in a mid-sized manufacturing plant. The date is present-day; the company has been profitable but is facing rising competition. The union recently won certification after a contentious campaign.
goal: Reach a tentative agreement on a first contract before the strike deadline at midnight. Success is signing a deal; failure is a strike or adjournment without agreement.
gm_notes: The union's bottom line: wage increase of at least 3%, improved safety language, and a just-cause provision. Management's bottom line: no more than 2% wage increase, flexible scheduling language, and a management-rights clause. Both sides have internal hardliners pushing for more extreme positions. The game master should introduce tensions: a leaked email, a walkout threat from the union's militant faction, or a last-minute demand from the company's lawyer. Pace toward the deadline.

roles.name: Union Lead Negotiator
roles.description: An experienced labor organizer who helped form the union. Pragmatic but must appease the militant faction.
roles.private_info: You suspect the company is bluffing about a lockout, but the union's strike fund is weak. You are willing to compromise on wages to get just-cause protection.

roles.name: Company Lead Negotiator
roles.description: A corporate lawyer hired by management, focused on cost control and maintaining flexibility.
roles.private_info: The CEO privately told you to avoid a strike at all costs, but you must appear tough to keep the board's confidence. You have room to offer 2.5% wages if needed.

roles.name: Union Militant
roles.description: A shop-floor leader popular with members, distrusts management and wants aggressive demands.
roles.private_info: You have secretly organized a walkout vote if the contract doesn't include a 5% wage hike. You don't know the union's true financial limits.

roles.name: Company HR Director
roles.description: Longtime employee, sympathetic to workers but loyal to management. Wants a deal but cautious.
roles.private_info: You know the company's safety record is worse than publicized; you fear a walkout could expose it. You are willing to concede on safety language.

npcs.name: Federal Mediator
npcs.description: A neutral government mediator brought in to facilitate. Patient and observant.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Wants a deal to avoid a strike that could disrupt local economy. Will suggest face-saving compromises.

npcs.name: Union Attorney
npcs.description: Young, aggressive lawyer representing the union. Likes to grandstand.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Wants to impress the union's parent organization. Will push for precedent-setting language even if it risks a deal.

npcs.name: Company CEO (via video call)
npcs.description: The distant CEO, appearing occasionally on screen. Gruff, impatient.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Considering relocating the plant overseas; a strike would give him the excuse. He wants either a cheap deal or a strike.

npcs.name: Union Treasurer
npcs.description: Retired accountant who volunteered; keeps the union's books.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Knows the strike fund covers only two weeks. Will privately urge the lead negotiator to settle.
